dialogue:
Xu Ze - Xu Xi / Feng An - Feng Jiaqi (Captain) / Heng Rui - Tu Hengrui / Wang Haoran - Zhong Haoran / Doctor Zhang - Zhang Cheng / Passerby - Shi Zhaoze  
At the edge of a cliff, the protagonist is running ahead, with two backpackers chasing behind. The protagonist looks panicked, runs to the cliff's edge, and leaps off.  

Doctor: I roughly understand your situation—insomnia caused by heavy psychological burden. I'll prescribe you a sedative, two tablets a day, no more than that. Since you still can't move on from this, I suggest you leave this city for a while.  

West Mountain, the protagonist starts hiking along the tourist trail.  

Passerby: Bro, do you know how to get to Xunyin Pavilion?  

Protagonist: Not far. Walk straight for about half an hour, there's a fork in the road. Take the left path for a bit, and you should be there.  

The further the protagonist goes, the more an eerie chill sets in. The surroundings grow quieter and quieter, until even the chirping of birds and insects can no longer be heard.  

Backpacker C: Are you alone? Otherwise, you won't be able to get out.  

The protagonist sits in the pavilion to rest. Backpacker A rests both hands on the railing.  

Backpacker A: Who are you hiding from? Found you. Too late.  

Suddenly, Backpacker B falls from above and crashes right in front of me. The protagonist screams and bolts away.  

The protagonist runs to the cliff's edge, turns back to glance at the pursuing backpackers, and leaps off.  

Hospital. The protagonist slowly opens their eyes. The doctor says they fainted on the mountain trail.  

The protagonist gets out of bed, quietly cracks the door open, and sees the two backpackers talking with the doctor.  

Doctor: What is your relationship with the injured person?  

Backpacker A: We're fairly close friends.  

Doctor: The injured person acted this way because they were experiencing hallucinations, caused by an overdose of Cardemicillin. Given that the patient has already taken an excessive amount of this drug and is currently in an unstable mental state, it's best if you keep your distance for now.

Under the city’s night sky, the bus stop sign stood alone. He stood on one side of the platform, his eyes vacant. Ever since that accident, his life had been shrouded in a layer of gray.  
His gaze was suddenly drawn by movement on the right—a little girl appeared in his line of sight. Her figure was blurry and translucent, as if she might vanish at any moment.  
At the front door of his home, he pushed it open and beckoned the nonexistent little girl inside. The living room was slightly messy, and the cans of alcohol in the corner revealed the turmoil inside him.  
At the center of the dining table, the little girl was lying on the left side. He handed her a cup of pudding.  
He began to clean up the cluttered house. The little girl’s figure lingered around him; she seemed to be smiling, encouraging him.  
When he stood in front of a certain door, he began to hesitate. His outstretched hand hung in the air, and in the end, he never opened that long-sealed door.  
One evening, he accidentally bumped into a cabinet beside him. A photo frame on the cabinet fell to the ground, shattering glass everywhere. He picked up the photo—it was a picture of him and the little girl together at the seaside.  
He suddenly realized that this little girl, who had been accompanying him all along, was his daughter. All the memories came flooding back like a tide.  
The next day, he stepped out of the house. The pudding cup on the table was already empty. He knew that from now on, he would no longer be alone. Because his daughter would always be in his heart, forever by his side.

User: Why is the inside of my hand red and white in patches  
AI: The most common cause of red and white patches on the inside of the hand is vascular function or circulation issues. When the palm skin is under pressure, capillary blood flow can be temporarily obstructed, forming white indentations. During anxiety or temperature changes, blood vessels in the hand may first constrict and then dilate, creating a red and white pattern. In cold weather or after contact with ice water, purplish-red or pale reticular patterns may appear. Fungal infections or irritation from chemicals such as hand sanitizers can lead to ring-shaped redness, scaling, and itching.  
It is recommended that you observe any changes and identify potential irritants. If the red and white patches persist for more than 1 day without fading, or are accompanied by itching, pain, peeling, or blisters, please visit a dermatology clinic.

User wants to know the release date of *Spider-Man 4*. *Spider-Man 4*, starring Tom Holland, has been scheduled and will be released in North America on July 31, 2026. The new film's subtitle has been set as *Spider-Man: Brand New Day*. Core cast: Tom Holland returns as the lead, with Destin Daniel Cretton serving as director. Zendaya and Jacob Batalon are expected to return. New additions include Jackie Chan's JC Stunt Team taking on the role of action directors. Charlie Cox's Daredevil is also expected to make a cameo. The film was previously planned for release on July 24, 2026, but Sony Pictures adjusted the date to July 31 to avoid competition with Christopher Nolan's new film *The Odyssey*.

User: Can drinking tea keep you awake?  
AI: Drinking tea can indeed affect sleep, and the key factor is caffeine. Black tea and Pu'er have relatively high levels, oolong tea is moderate, while green tea and white tea are relatively low. If you're prone to insomnia, try to avoid tea after 4 PM. Opt for highly fermented teas like aged white tea or ripe Pu'er. For the first infusion, steep it in hot water for 30 seconds and then discard the liquid to remove some of the caffeine.  

User: What about Monster energy drinks?  
AI: Drinking Monster makes it even harder to fall asleep than tea. A single can of Monster typically contains between 140 and 160 milligrams of caffeine. It starts taking effect within 30 minutes of consumption, and the state of heightened alertness can last 3 to 4 hours. Stop drinking it at least 6 hours before bedtime. The recommended daily caffeine intake for adults is no more than 400 milligrams.  

User: What about milk?  
AI: Drinking milk not only won't keep you awake, but it's often recommended as a sleep aid. The tryptophan in milk is a precursor for synthesizing serotonin and melatonin. Drinking a small cup of warm milk, about 150 to 200 ml, an hour before bed works best.  

User: What about soda?  
AI: Unlike milk, soda is a typical sleep disruptor. Caffeinated sodas, like cola, contain caffeine that acts as a central nervous system stimulant, directly suppressing drowsiness. For caffeine-free sugary sodas, like Sprite or Fanta, the high sugar content causes blood sugar spikes and crashes, and people who consume high amounts of sugar tend to have lighter nighttime sleep. It's best to finish drinking soda 3 to 4 hours before bed, and limit a single serving to under 200 milliliters.
